rationale: Both abstracts define a specific randomized head-to-head gap and fit an EP ablation protocol topic. Neither reports outcome effect sizes, comparators with CI, or p values, so results_rigor is necessarily low. Y is stronger overall because it explicitly identifies the paper as a protocol, separates trial status from outcome results, and states that no efficacy or safety results are reported. Its conclusion is more calibrated to the study design. X has slightly better Europace-style headings, but its conclusion overreaches by saying the trial is positioned to clarify the optimal ablation strategy before results are available.
loser_weakness: X most lacked certainty calibration: it presented a protocol abstract with no outcome data, yet used stronger practice-informing language than the design and available results support.
